danger_signals.visual_form: Awkwardly positioned spike obstacles throughout levels
danger_signals.meaning: Instant kill hazard; environmental threat requiring precise navigation

danger_signals.visual_form: Circus-themed antagonists (implied by Psycho Circus gang context)
danger_signals.meaning: Hostile entities requiring avoidance or combat engagement
World-state corruption: funpark sabotaged, civilians in distress, mission urgency

safety_signals.visual_form: Amusement park environment with roller coasters, hoops, playful architecture
safety_signals.meaning: Home territory; safe-zone aesthetic despite danger; player's goal location
Resource availability; player empowerment through collection
Reward state; safe respite from main hazards upon point threshold

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Circus funpark; safe zones; performer rescue missions

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Psycho Circus gang branding; sabotage imagery
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Zero the Kamikaze Squirrel (allied enemy character)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Sabotaged funpark; corrupted circus spaces; industrial takeover aesthetic
Trapped within funpark levels; kidnapped state

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Funpark as thriving amusement center; Edgar banned after prank incident
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Circus operates normally; performers safe and performing

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Funpark corrupted by Psycho Circus occupation; traps and spikes integrated into environment; performers kidnapped
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Hostile enemies populate levels; roller coasters become traversal challenges; exit warps blocked until tasks completed

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Hoop passages open; platform sequences collapse after use; exit warp revealed
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player completes objectives (pass hoops, step platforms, ride coasters); progression gated by environmental puzzle-solving

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Rescued performers visible; Aeriel (girlfriend) freed; funpark restored
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Edgar's schemes stopped; Psycho Circus defeated; normal circus operations resume

environmental_storytelling.example: Funpark designed for amusement (roller coasters, hoops) now weaponized with spike traps; safe entertainment spaces become deadly
Psycho Circus gang occupies carnival spaces; Zero the Kamikaze Squirrel and associates patrol sabotaged attractions
Kidnapped circus performers appear as trapped NPCs within levels; player sees direct consequence of Edgar's villainy
Exit warps remain hidden until environmental objectives complete; world refuses progression until player solves funpark-themed puzzles (hoop passages, platform sequences, coaster rides)
Aero's red bat silhouette identifies protagonist; acrobatic abilities match circus identity; enemies visually distinct as outsiders/invaders

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Shoots limited stars; performs aerial diagonal drill attack; explores levels seeking tasks and objectives
Dies instantly upon spike contact; knocked back by enemy attacks; cannot progress without task completion
Remain trapped within level spaces; visible but unreachable until rescue achieved; signal mission critical status
Orchestrates sabotage through Psycho Circus gang; maintains kidnapped performers; controls funpark corruption (off-screen direction of enemy behavior)
Populate levels as hostile enemies; defend sabotaged attractions; attack Aero on sight

notes: Aero the Acro-Bat communicates world-state primarily through environmental corruption: the funpark transforms from safe amusement space to hostile sabotaged zone. Danger is visualized through spike placement, enemy occupation, and kidnapped performers. Faction identity relies on circus theming for protagonists and carnival-invasion aesthetic for antagonists. The game lacks explicit color-faction systems or symbolic heraldry; instead, it uses thematic environmental design (amusement-to-peril transformation) as its primary manifestation layer. Safety and danger exist on a spectrum defined by level architecture rather than discrete visual markers. Task-gating (hoops, platforms, coasters) becomes a world-state signal: incomplete tasks = blocked progression = world still corrupted.
